annotation.personal_flag: September sweet spot: sperm whales still active in the channel, empty coastal paths, volcanic silence — and nearly two-thirds cheaper than August.
annotation.raw_reasoning: September 17-24 sits in the ideal post-peak window. August school holidays are over across Europe; domestic Portuguese tourism drops sharply. Sperm whales are resident year-round in the Pico-Faial channel and September encounters remain excellent — the migratory baleen species (blue, fin, humpback) have largely passed but sperm whales compensate. Weather: Sept on Pico averages 22C, with rain risk increasing vs summer but still predominantly good weather. Some Atlantic fronts possible but week-scale windows are reliable. Crowds: very low — accommodation prices drop, trails empty, boat tours often half-full. €318 is exceptional — well below any historical norm I'd expect for this route (first scan, thin baseline). Isolation score 5: genuinely off-season feel.

notable_events: Sea turtle nesting still active in September (season runs July-October) — hatching begins for early nests
annotation.personal_flag: Early September on Sal: turtles still nesting and early-season hatchlings emerging, seas calm and clear, and virtually empty beaches — the dry season's best value window at €282.
annotation.raw_reasoning: September 10-15 on Sal. The dry season continues through September. Sea turtle nesting active: late-July nests begin hatching in September, so the beach wildlife cycle adds hatchling emergence to ongoing nesting — a rare wildlife double. Weather: slightly lower temps (26-27C), same dry and sunny conditions. European crowds: schools back in session across Europe, resort occupancy drops sharply after first week of September. Santa Maria beach hotels running at ~40-50% vs 80%+ in December-March peak. Isolation score 4: shoulder season creates genuine quietness on an island that is normally resort-heavy. Route via Milan (MXP) is unusual but both legs are easyJet — clean 1-stop each way with 5h layovers within limit. €282 is outstanding — no historical baseline to compare but this price is well below any comparable Atlantic island route. 5 nights (Sep 10-15) fits minimum stay.

notable_events: August is the single best month for loggerhead turtle nesting on Sal — nightly nest counts at maximum
annotation.personal_flag: August is peak turtle nesting on Sal: highest nest counts of the year, excellent diving, warm flat sea — and still less crowded than December resort season.
annotation.raw_reasoning: August 11-19 on Sal. Dry season at its most reliable. Loggerhead nesting: Project Biodiversity identifies August as the optimum month for nesting density — Sal's tallest beaches see highest nesting counts. Turtle night-watch programs run every night with guided groups. Weather: avg 28C, flat seas, near-zero rain chance. Diving: warm water 27-28C, typically 20-30m visibility off Pedra de Lume and Buracona. Crowds: European school summer holidays mean more visitors than shoulder months, but total visitor density is well below the December-March European beach escape peak. Route via LPA (Gran Canaria) is clean — 6.75h total travel. Isolation score 3: Sal is the most developed Cape Verde island (beach resort strip in Santa Maria) but only ~30km long — easy to escape to empty northern coast. €577 reasonable for this route.

notable_events: Festival Cais Agosto (Jul 29 - Aug 2, Sao Roque do Pico): 5-day music and cultural festival coincides with arrival date
annotation.distortion_reason: Festival Cais Agosto (Jul 29-Aug 2) runs concurrent with arrival — elevated accommodation pressure and local crowds in Sao Roque do Pico for days 1-4 of the trip. Not a structural baseline price signal.
annotation.personal_flag: Lands right on Festival Cais Agosto — five days of local music on an island already buzzing with peak whale-watching activity; exciting but not the isolated Atlantic escape.
annotation.raw_reasoning: July 29 arrival on Pico coincides exactly with the start of Festival Cais Agosto (Jul 29-Aug 2), a growing cultural/music festival in Sao Roque do Pico. This creates real accommodation pressure and crowd distortion that is event-driven, not structural. Whale watching is excellent (peak sperm whale season). Summer weather is stable — 24-26C, calmer seas. Crowds are at medium baseline for July but the festival pushes local density up. The €778 price may partly reflect this event period. Mark as distorted — do not use for baseline comparison. Isolation score 3: festival + summer = busy pockets, though the volcanic interior remains empty.

notable_events: Peak loggerhead sea turtle nesting period (Jul-Aug, Santa Maria beach guided night watches available nightly)
annotation.personal_flag: Late July on Sal: highest nesting density of the year for loggerheads, warm flat seas, and fewer Northern Europeans than the December-March peak.
annotation.raw_reasoning: July 29-Aug 5 on Sal Island, Cape Verde. The dry season runs roughly June-October, making July weather excellent: 27-29C, low humidity, calm seas. Loggerhead sea turtle nesting peaks in July-August on Santa Maria beach — Project Biodiversity recorded 36,500+ nests in 2024 across Sal and Boavista. Turtle-watching tours are well-organized and regulated but have become increasingly popular with tourist groups. Crowds: European summer tourists arrive, but Sal's peak tourist season for Europeans is December-March (beach holiday escape from cold); July is lower than that peak though higher than autumn. Isolation score 3: Santa Maria has resort infrastructure but the eastern and northern coastlines remain quiet. €837 (lastminute) is higher than Kiwi alternatives for similar routes — within 21-day window premium. Diving conditions excellent in July (warm water, 25m+ visibility).

notable_events: Bom Jesus Festival (Aug 6, just before departure)
Semana dos Baleeiros / Whalers Week (late August, Aug 24-31 — after return)
annotation.personal_flag: Peak August on Pico: guaranteed daily whale sightings, warm ocean, but accommodation full and prices structurally elevated for the season — no good deal here.
annotation.raw_reasoning: August 7-17 is core peak summer on Pico Island. Bom Jesus Festival Aug 6 (day before departure) may affect availability but ends before travel. Whalers Week (Aug 24-31) is after return — no direct overlap. High crowds structurally: Portuguese and EU summer holiday peak. Whale watching is excellent (sperm whales, bottlenose dolphins, common dolphins all active). Weather: reliable summer — avg 25C, low rain probability. Price €1,016 reflects structural peak-season pricing, not event distortion. Isolation score 2: Madalena busy with ferries, beaches crowded, trails busy. Lajes do Pico has whale-watching tour queues.
